title: hieracium murorum subsp. flavescens
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. flavescens, also known as yellow hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that grows in mountainous regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, Hieracium murorum subsp. flavescens is often used in infusions, teas, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
